title: Technical Problem Solving, Coding, and Work-Sample Boundaries · Hiring Practice Room
summary: A guided career studio extends observable technical interview practice system while retaining truthful evidence, accessibility, privacy, security, consent, and learner agency. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of observable technical interview practice system while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.
Externalize assumptions, alternatives, invariants, tradeoffs, checkpoints, uncertainty, and help requests without narrating every keystroke.
Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Problem Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The workshop variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Problem Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Problem Contract, keep Problem Contract connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The workshop variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Problem Contract condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GitHub Resume and Project Presentation Tutorial — Controls selective project presentation, useful repository descriptions, setup, demonstration, tests, dependency currency, and truthful links from application evidence.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Problem Contract, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Problem Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Problem Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Beginning to code immediately demonstrates seniority.

options.text: Keep the plausible Sample Boundary output and hide the violated observable technical interview practice system assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Sample Boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Sample Boundary without its stated preconditions, omit the observable technical interview practice system trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Sample Boundary for observable technical interview practice system.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Sample Boundary condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Sample Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Sample Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The workshop variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Sample Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieving Sample Boundary through the concrete observable technical interview practice system case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Sample Boundary, keep Sample Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Sample Boundary, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Sample Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Sample Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The workshop variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Sample Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Every interview assignment is reasonable because participation is optional.
